summary: Round 3 generator delivered a strong, structurally diverse batch dominated by OI_Price-channel ports of proven pricechange templates, novel exit-family experiments addressing the R2 X-pipeline starvation gap, and short-side mean-reversion fades that finally break from the continuation-only short cluster. Inclusive-default posture applied: 20/21 accepted (some with watch-flags for trap geometry per metric_critic), 1 revised due to a parser-level multi-value OR limitation on the weekday field, 0 rejected. Coverage gaps filled: OI_Price/long alpha porting (4 archetypes), short-side mean-reversion (3), exit-family innovation (5), session/weekday regime gating, basis dislocation, cross-channel tight-window timing, and a kelly-safe sizing risk filter.

accepted_archetypes.notes: OI burst with aggressive taker buying in high-liquidity names — port of E126 winning template to OI_Price channel where 61 archetypes registered but none in top-10.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; fills OI_Price top-10 gap. Metric_critic flags high trap risk (E126-family asymmetric TP/SL geometry) — GPU sweep should constrain SL<=2.5x TP and validate via kelly_capped/p25_capped_tail, not legacy p25.

accepted_archetypes.notes: OI burst on names where global short ratio is elevated — squeeze fuel hypothesis, mirrors E148 from pricechange to OI channel.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; squeeze-fuel logic ported to OI_Price is structurally novel. Devil notes funding-rate staleness and channel-semantics mismatch — track per-symbol concentration and time-since-funding-update during paper-shadow.

accepted_archetypes.notes: OI reduction with concurrent taker BUY pressure — short-cover reversal setup distinct from continuation longs.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch (tight ~85 trig); first divergence/reversal entry on OI_Price. Metric_critic flags counter-trend geometry concern — pair with fixed/breakeven exits and force SL<=1.5x TP in variant grid.

accepted_archetypes.notes: OI burst with neutral funding (no crowding) plus extreme taker buying — clean signal without funding skew.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; funding-neutral gate is the cleanest direct attack on the R2 E126 funding-skew trap. Devil rightly notes the band is narrow — confirm with ablation at |funding|<=3bp during paper-shadow.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Triple-AND combining the three top R2 winners (taker_buy + high_liq + global_short_high) into one selective entry.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; triple-intersection of E126/E051/E148 components. Metric_critic flags high trap risk because all three parents share asymmetric pump-long geometry — must validate with kelly_capped, and overlap-rate vs E126/E051/E148 should stay <80% to confirm marginal value.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Crowded-long pump with positive funding and taker SELL dominance — classic mean-reversion fade with natural SL=TP symmetry.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; first true mean-reversion short on pricechange (existing 94 shorts collapsed to baseline because they reused continuation logic). Geometry is structurally symmetric per metric_critic, lower trap risk than other shorts.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Smart money positioned short while retail funding extreme — high-conviction fade short.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; smart-money + funding-extreme cross-signal is novel for short side. Devil flags funding-snapshot staleness (8h cycle) — bucket trades by snapshot age during shadow.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Crash with retail capitulation extreme + early taker buy reversal — counter-trend bounce.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch (tight ~45 trig per quant). High trap risk per metric_critic — counter-trend longs structurally invite asymmetric SL>>TP. Force SL<=2x TP and require TP>=0.8% in variant grid.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Single-filter Reserved6 long — minimal AND to preserve sparse R6 trigger count (~120 baseline).
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; pattern_miner architectural fix for R6 over-specification. Lowest trap risk in batch per metric_critic (R6 geometry is more directional than pump channels).

accepted_archetypes.notes: R6 long gated only by high liquidity — sparse-channel safe filter.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; devil correctly notes liquidity_bucket alone has no directional bias and risks regression to channel/side baseline (R1 silent-fallthrough echo). Compare lift vs plain R6/long baseline — kill if delta<+0.05.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Session-gated entry restricting pricechange longs to US trading hours — exploits liquidity regime.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; first archetype to use the supported `session` field. Ample expected sample (~340). Devil flags potential macro-event overfit in 30d window — bucket by sub-window during shadow.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Pump with extreme basis AND premium — perp dislocation fade.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; lowest trap risk among shorts per metric_critic — bounded mean-reversion thesis with naturally symmetric geometry. Verify Spearman(basis_rate, premium_bps) on triggers <0.85 to confirm joint-signal novelty.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Pure time_only 60s exit paired with high-conviction entries — quick mean-reversion harvest before drift.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; time_only family has 0/2745 prior positives, but pattern_miner flagged the mean-reversion pairing as untested. Treat as deliberate hypothesis test — require mean AND p25_capped_tail both >0 before any keep-promotion.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Trail family with 2.0% step on top entries — wider trail to avoid premature stop-out.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; trail family max≈-0.006 suggests parameter mis-tune not dead concept (pattern_miner). Sweep step in {1.5,2.0,2.5}% with activation_threshold to map the surface.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Two-step TP ladder 60/40 with first TP at 0.5% second at 1.5% — captures bulk early.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; multi_tp prior is brutal (0/3111 positive), but front-loaded 60/40 shape attacks the residual-leg failure mode. Devil flag: novel_dim is entry-side filter on exit archetype — verify kernel evaluates it correctly. Track residual-leg PnL in isolation.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Breakeven kernel with stricter trigger threshold and 30s activation — differentiates from X100 floor.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; quant correctly flagged that BE-timing parameters live in the exit-config slot, not novel_dim — ensure be_trigger_pct and be_activation_seconds are both swept to avoid collapse onto X100.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Hybrid: take 50% at fixed 0.5% TP, trail remaining 50% with 1.5% step — symmetric risk/reward.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; genuinely new hybrid family but requires kernel composition support — none of the 5 live kernels natively combine fixed+trail. If kernel unavailable, downgrade to multi_tp approximation rather than skip.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Pricechange and OI_Price both fire within 60 seconds with taker_buy confirmation — tight double-channel timing window.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch; quant correctly notes the 60s timing window is not expressible in filter DSL and effectively reduces to a 3-condition AND. The temporal join must be enforced at kernel/event-pairing level, not filter level — flag for implementation review.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Three-channel alignment requiring funding-neutral regime — avoids crowded-trade traps.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted with watch (tight ~60 trig); first cc_* with funding-neutral gate. Lowest trap risk in cc batch per metric_critic — funding-neutral filter explicitly excludes the regime that produced the E126 trap.

accepted_archetypes.notes: Risk archetype enforcing position size only when both liquidity and taker_buy confirm — kelly-safe sizing.
accepted_archetypes.synthesizer_note: Accepted; first risk archetype combining liquidity+taker_buy as size-gating AND. Devil correctly flags side=both is asymmetric in practice (taker_buy>=0.60 clusters on long regimes) — log per-side trigger counts during shadow to confirm short coverage is real.

revised_archetypes.revision_reason: Quant flagged that the original 5-value OR list (weekday=Mon|Tue|Wed|Thu|Fri) is not standard parser semantics — the filter parser will AND-conjunct these into 'weekday=Mon AND weekday=Tue AND ...' which evaluates to zero matches, OR it will use only the last value. Either way the intent (exclude weekends) is not actually enforced.
revised_archetypes.revised_notes: Single mid-week (Wed) gate as a minimal test of the weekday dimension on a parser that doesn't support multi-value OR. If Wed shows positive lift_vs_baseline, follow-up Round 4 archetypes can probe Tue/Thu/Fri individually to characterize the full weekday surface; if the parser is later extended with OR/IN semantics, restore the original weekend-avoid formulation.

next_round_focus: Round 4 should (a) propose explicit symmetric-geometry exits (TP=SL or TP/SL ratio constrained to [0.5,1.5]) to attack the E126 trap structurally rather than via metric penalization, (b) extend the cross-channel chain primitives — current parser cannot enforce <60s timing windows, so several cc_* archetypes silently degrade to flat ANDs, and (c) add OR/IN semantics to the weekday/session fields so multi-value regime gates are first-class rather than collapsing to single-value tests.
